marketState: neutral, fair-valued spot with a bullish weather skew building into weeks 2-3
summary: NG=F is trading almost exactly at modeled fair value across all horizons (now, +7D, +14D, +21D all show FAIR status with spreads under $0.10), so there is no valuation dislocation to lean on. The dominant signal is weather: 8-16 day demand is running +35% above the 5-year norm and above the historical max range, while 1-7 day demand is a more modest +5.6% above normal - a heat wave building rather than peaking now. Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, a crowded short that could fuel a squeeze if the heat materializes into stronger power burn. Offsetting this, storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, and seasonality reliability is explicitly flagged Low (59pp spread) with a mixed/slightly negative 4-week typical move (-1.4%). Technicals are split: intraday (5m/15m) has turned bullish and 4h is overbought (above upper Bollinger), but daily and weekly trends remain bearish (50<200 EMA, RSI 39-42) - consistent with a potential short-term bounce inside a longer sideways-to-soft trend; futures curve data was unavailable this run.

longTerm.rationale: Extreme 8-16 day heat demand (+35% vs 5y, above historical max) and a heavily crowded managed-money short (8th percentile of 5y) both argue for upside risk skew even though price sits right at fair value; the fair-value curve itself drifts up from $2.923 now to $3.016 by +21D, implying the model already expects some seasonal firming.
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D heat forecast fades back toward normal, storage keeps printing bearish surprises, or price closes below the ~$2.65 stop / lower fair-value band

shortTerm.rationale: Price is dead-on fair value at both front and next contracts (spreads inside $0.03), 1-7D weather is only modestly above normal (+5.6%), and technicals conflict: 4h is overbought (above upper Bollinger, RSI ~65) while daily/weekly trend is still bearish (EMA 50<200, RSI 39-42) - no clean short-term edge, and futures-curve/contango data was missing this run.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $2.75 with heat forecasts easing would flip the near-term bias bearish instead
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback to $2.80-$2.85 with RSI cooling (better long entry) or a daily EMA bullish cross confirming trend before chasing longs into overbought 4h conditions

keyDrivers: 8-16 day cooling demand +35% vs 5y and above historical max - building heat wave
Managed money net short at 8th percentile of 5y range - crowded positioning, squeeze risk
Price sits almost exactly at fair value across all horizons - no valuation edge either way
Storage +180 Bcf above 5y with bearish +22 Bcf build surprise caps upside
Daily/weekly technicals still bearish while intraday/4h is overbought
risks: 8-16 day weather forecasts are inherently uncertain and can revert quickly
Seasonality reliability flagged Low (59pp spread) - historical pattern not trustworthy this week
